event: Fairy tale vision of the Garden of Eden
symbols: lush garden with animals — the peaceable kingdom of Eden before the Fall
horses and creatures coexisting — the harmony of creation in its unfallen state
luxuriant trees — the abundant provision of God's garden where every tree was pleasing to the eye
description: A dreamy watercolor painting depicting a fantastical garden scene. Luxuriant trees with colorful foliage — blue, green, golden — fill the composition. Several animals inhabit the garden, including horses of different colors in the foreground, what appears to be a fox or reddish animal running through the middle ground, and a dark seated figure (possibly human) at the far left. The palette is soft and ethereal with pastel blues, pinks, greens, and golden yellows. The style is modernist and fairy-tale-like, with loose, flowing watercolor washes creating a dreamlike atmosphere.
theological_description: Martiros Sarian's watercolor evokes the Garden of Eden described in Genesis 2:8-9: 'The Lord God had planted a garden in the east, in Eden; and there He put the man He had formed. The Lord God made all kinds of trees grow out of the ground — trees that were pleasing to the eye and good for food.' The artist's vision of paradise emphasizes the beauty and harmony of the original creation, where animals coexisted peacefully and the natural world displayed unfallen splendor. The varied animals moving freely through the garden recall Genesis 2:19-20, where God brought the animals to Adam to name them, establishing humanity's role as caretaker of creation. The dreamlike quality of the painting captures the sense of paradise as a memory or longing — what Ecclesiastes 3:11 calls the eternity God has 'set in the human heart,' the intuition that the world was once and will again be whole. The lush abundance reflects the prophetic vision of restoration in Isaiah 51:3: 'The Lord will surely comfort Zion... He will make her wilderness like Eden, her desert like the garden of the Lord.'

scripture_refs.reason: God planting a garden in Eden with trees pleasing to the eye — the lush paradise depicted
God bringing animals to Adam in the garden — the diverse creatures inhabiting this Eden
God making the wilderness like Eden — the prophetic hope of paradise restored
Eternity set in the human heart — the dreamlike longing for paradise that this painting evokes
